text:
REGIME — Broad risk-on, small caps led, tech surged, staples/utilities lagged.

INDICES — VIX 18.8, falling. IWM +1.44%, QQQ +1.03%, SPY +0.98%, DIA +0.60% — small caps outperformed large caps across the board. WTI oil (USO) was the standout cross-asset move, rising +2.92% on Middle East/Iran deal optimism, while VIXY dropped -3.80%.

SECTORS — XLK +2.10%, XLF +1.75%, XLY +0.91%, XLC +0.76%, XLI +0.71%, XLRE +0.47%, XLV +0.45%, XLB +0.44%, XLE +0.30%, XLP -1.00%, XLU -1.21%. Top industries: WGMI +5.85% and WCLD +5.44%; bottom: SLV -1.16% and XLU -1.21%.

THE 7s — Software +5.46%, Cloud +5.61%, Toll Road +2.76%, New Metals +2.65%, Medtech +2.11%, Insurance +2.01%, Semi +1.91%, Buildout +1.59%, New Staples +1.55%, Bank +1.18%, Mag +1.02%, Defense +1.00%, Fab +0.78%, Housing +0.72%, Industrial +0.42%, Energy +0.37%, Health +0.20%, Freight +0.09%, Power -0.59%, Old Staples -0.88%, Old Metals -0.91%. Strongest ticker in Software 7: ORCL +12.69%. Weakest in Old Metals 7: SLV proxy dragging the basket.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC Apr 29, 2026; CME shows hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%. Fed stance: 6 hawks (Cook, Hammack, Kashkari, Logan, Barkin, Goolsbee), 5 neutral, 4 doves. USD liquidity stress at 54/100 (Elevated); most-stressed component is Reserve Scarcity at 80th percentile, with CP-T-Bill Spread close behind at 79th percentile. ON-RRP near zero at $0.2B; TGA fell $99.3B.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 60.9, Greed. Top movers: ORCL +12.69% (Software), NOW +8.73% (Software), GS -1.87% (Bank). 20d momentum: best sector XLK +6.57%, worst XLP -3.22%; best basket Semi +22.48%, worst Software -10.88%. ORCL surged on AI-powered cloud upgrades announced alongside strong TSMC results boosting the broader tech bid.

WATCH — ORCL: +12.69%, 2.2x vol spike; gap extension risk tomorrow, watch for mean reversion or continuation above $240. GS: -1.87%, 2.1x vol with financials broadly up +1.75%; divergence ahead of earnings next week flags potential catalyst risk. LIT: +2.72%, 3.0x vol spike; New Metals basket +2.65% on 20d momentum of +11.43% — watch for sustained breakout or reversal on battery-metals sentiment.
